text: This study aims to know the challenges faced by a judge in trying to uphold the values of certainty of law, justice, and utilitarianism in the context of Indonesian civil courts. Based on the method of normative juridical research and empirical evidence collected in the field, the study found obstacles falling into two categories. They are (i) the obstacles from within the persons of judicial personnel namely related to the system of appointing personnel of the judiciary, the academic level of education of law graduated, the competence of a legal science that has been acquired, the value of morality, and the degree of welfare. (ii) Obstacles coming from the environment outside, namely in the form of ensuring that the judiciary has been truly independent, that the formulation of the laws and regulations are always of the quality of legislation, a rule of administration judiciary that functions well, and the people’s involvement in legal procedures as well as sufficient checking on judges at the courtroom and outside of it. The author of this research concludes that the overhaul of the judiciary which aims at synergy between the certainty of law and social justice necessarily requires reform of judges within the Indonesian courts of law. The key elements in this context are removing unlawful connections from the appointments processes, continuous judicial education and a better quality of life and sufficient physical protection of the courts to insulate them from any external influences. A combination of three elements such as the competences, morality and institutional framework in itself is decisive in the high quality of the judges’ judgement and decision.
